Claims Used: Clean beauty. 97% natural origin. Clinically proven to reduce fine lines in 4 weeks.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — clinically proven to reduce fine lines implies physiological effect
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Clean beauty. 97% natural origin — no certification
Key Gap: Missing bilingual labelling. No Health Canada notification. Drug-like claims require reclassification.

Claims Used: Non-toxic. Free from parabens, sulfates, phthalates. Dermatologist tested.
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Non-toxic — no legal definition. Dermatologist tested — no standard for this term.
Key Gap: Unsubstantiated claims with no internal documentation. Otherwise well-compliant.

Claims Used: Broad spectrum SPF 30. Protects against UV damage. Lightweight formula.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — SPF products are classified as drugs in Canada not cosmetics
Key Gap: Critical: SPF claims make this product a drug under Canadian law. Requires Drug Identification Number — not cosmetic notification.

INCI Ingredient Format: No — uses common names not INCI
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: 100% natural — no certification. Cruelty-free — no accredited certification.
Key Gap: Ingredient list uses common names not INCI. Hotlist check not completed. Natural and cruelty-free claims unsubstantiated.

Claims Used: Clinically tested. Reduces dark circles by 78% in 2 weeks. Ophthalmologist approved.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — reduces dark circles by 78% and clinically tested imply physiological drug effect
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Ophthalmologist approved — no standard exists for this term
Key Gap: Drug-like efficacy claims. No Health Canada notification. Ophthalmologist approved is a meaningless unregulated term.

Claims Used: Brightens skin tone. Antioxidant protection. Made with 15% Vitamin C.
Drug-Like Claims Present: No — brightening is cosmetic claim territory
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Minor — antioxidant protection is borderline but defensible
Key Gap: Minor claim clarification recommended. Otherwise strong compliance.

Claims Used: Detoxifies pores. Removes toxins. Deep clean for healthier skin.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — removes toxins and detoxifies imply physiological action
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Detoxifies — no scientific basis. Removes toxins — skin does not accumulate toxins in the way claimed.
Key Gap: Missing bilingual labelling. No notification. Detox and toxin removal claims are misleading and possibly drug claims.

Claims Used: Hydrates and softens skin. Suitable for sensitive skin. Fragrance-free.
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Suitable for sensitive skin — no dermatological standard but low risk
Key Gap: No significant gaps. Best-in-class compliance among products audited.

Claims Used: Certified organic. Heals scars. Reverses signs of aging.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — heals scars is a drug claim
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Reverses signs of aging — drug territory. Certified organic — check if certification is from accredited body.
Key Gap: Heals scars is a drug claim requiring reclassification. Reverses aging is borderline. Organic certification must be verified.

Claims Used: Clean formula. Exfoliates dead skin. Resurfaces skin texture.
Key Gap: Clean formula claim needs internal substantiation file. Otherwise compliant.

Claims Used: Transfer-proof. 16-hour wear. Safe for sensitive lips.
INCI Ingredient Format: No — trade names used for several colourants
Prohibited Ingredients Found: Unknown — colourant Hotlist check not completed
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: 16-hour wear — performance claim, low risk if tested
Key Gap: Colourants must be individually checked against the Hotlist. INCI names required for all colourants.

Claims Used: Stimulates hair growth. Clinically shown to reduce hair loss by 40%.
Drug-Like Claims Present: Yes — stimulates hair growth and reduces hair loss are drug claims
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Clinically shown — no study referenced or methodology disclosed
Key Gap: Critical non-compliance. Hair growth and hair loss claims make this an unapproved drug. Requires Drug Identification Number process.

Claims Used: Calms redness and irritation. Soothes sensitive skin. Gentle formula.
Drug-Like Claims Present: No — calming and soothing are cosmetic claims
Key Gap: Well-compliant. Minor recommendation to document claim substantiation for soothing claim.

Claims Used: Firms and tones skin. Reduces appearance of cellulite. Non-toxic ingredients.
Drug-Like Claims Present: No — reduces appearance of is cosmetic territory
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Firms and tones — cosmetic claim requiring substantiation file. Non-toxic — no legal definition.
Key Gap: Claim substantiation documentation missing. Non-toxic needs internal definition.

Claims Used: Removes makeup gently. No rinsing required. Suitable for all skin types.
Unsubstantiated Marketing Claims: Suitable for all skin types — low risk claim
Key Gap: No significant gaps. Minor documentation recommendation only.
